statement.markdown: Your task is to fix the function `flip_and_count` to correctly flip all the bits in a given array `nums` and then count the bits that were flipped.
statement.inputFormat: The first line will contain the size of the input array. The second line will contain the input array
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the count of the bits that were flipped.
statement.notes: The input array may contain negative numbers.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool flip_and_count(int n) { int ans = 0; while (n) { ans += n & 1; n >>= 1; } return ans; } };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean flip_and_count(int n) { int ans = 0; while (n) { ans += n & 1; n >>= 1; } return true; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def flip_and_count(self, n: int) -> bool: ans = 0 while n: ans += n & 1 n >>= 1 return bool(ans)
starterCode.javascript: function flip_and_count(n) { let ans = 0 while (n) { ans += n & 1 n >>= 1 } return ans; }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool flip_and_count(int n) { int ans = 0; while (n) { ans += n & 1; n >>= 1; if (!ans) break; } return ans; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean flip_and_count(int n) { int ans = 0; while (n) { ans += n & 1; n >>= 1; if (!ans) break; } return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def flip_and_count(self, n: int) -> bool: ans = 0 while n: ans += n & 1 n >>= 1 if not ans: break return bool(ans)
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function flip_and_count(n) { let ans = 0 while (n) { ans += n & 1 n >>= 1 if (!ans) break; } return ans; }
